text:
Market today: Cautious-to-neutral mood. The real driver for your names is AI spending momentum - the 'follow the money into hardware' fund manager note and AI agents headline reinforce that chip/infra names stay bid, but nothing here is a fresh catalyst. Google's $4.7B EU fine adds mild regulatory overhang on Big Tech broadly.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the one screaming for attention - that's a 3x leveraged semiconductor ETF and decay is eating you alive on margin; stop is $200, price is $217, not far off. SPAL (-13.72%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO - it's already near its stop at $21.38 vs $21.70 price; margin amplifies this pain fast. SMH (-2.5%) is milder but worth watching as the unleveraged semis read.

Watch: (1) Whether SOXL holds $200 - a close below triggers your stop, no debate. (2) SMH direction - it leads SOXL and tells you if semis are stabilizing.

Bottom line: Your two leveraged losers are the only real job today - protect capital there before anything else.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - honor it, no exceptions. Leveraged decay plus margin is a brutal combo if semis keep sliding; no reason to widen here.
SPAL: Stop $21.38 is basically right here at $21.70. Set it as a hard order now - brand-new leveraged ETF with fast moves and you're already down 13.7% on margin.
No new buys today - nothing in the scanner has a fresh catalyst strong enough to justify adding risk given two leveraged positions already bleeding.
